item.title: Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.

item.title: Gemini Spark, Google’s agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google's 24/7 agentic assistant, Gemini Spark, comes to Mac alongside other improvements, like real-time tracking and support for more apps.

item.title: Sony to end physical PlayStation game disc production in 2028
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Sony will stop producing physical discs for all new PlayStation games beginning in 2028, as the company embraces an all-digital future.

item.title: Builders Stage agenda revealed: Practical strategies for scaling startups at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: The Builders Stage is returning to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, bringing together 10,000+ founders, startup operators, and investors for practical conversations. and Q&A on what it takes to build and scale successful companies. Register now to save up to $330.

item.title: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Meta is developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business, selling access to AI compute power and models. The move would pit it against the big cloud providers like Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

item.title: The ‘Father of the Internet’ is finally retiring
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.

item.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.

item.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Wayve’s offering is part of a growing trend of AI startups using employee tenders as a strategic tool to attract and retain talent.

item.title: Startup Battlefield Australia application closes in days: Apply before July 6
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: What if one pitch changed everything? The next company nobody has heard of yet is building something that will matter. It could be yours.

item.title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The free open source agentic program is finally invading your phone.

item.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

item.title: Realta Fusion generates electricity directly from a fusion reaction, an apparent first
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: “We can take power from a plasma,” Kieran Furlong, co-founder and CEO of Realta Fusion, told TechCrunch. The milestone shows “what’s possible,” he added.

item.title: Google introduces a faster, cheaper image generator with Nano Banana 2 Lite
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Google is updating its image generator to make it faster and cheaper, making it a more useful tool for creators looking to make AI content.

item.title: Clicks shows off its BlackBerry-inspired phone in a new hands-on video
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A new video shows the final production version of the upcoming Clicks Communicator, a BlackBerry-like smartphone that runs modern apps.

item.title: Nvidia competitor Etched hits $5B valuation, $1B in sales for AI chip
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Nvidia AI chip competitor Etched says it has already booked $1 billion under contract for the inference systems powered by its chip.

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

item.title: Acti puts AI agents directly into your smartphone keyboard
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Acti is betting the smartphone keyboard is the next home for AI assistants. The startup's new keyboard for iOS and Android works across apps and lets users create custom AI-powered shortcuts using natural language.

item.title: Threads adds new features to Live Chats as it expands access
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The updates include translations, new tools for hosts, and more.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Science is a workbench that gives scientists one environment to do computational research, saving them from the need to bounce between databases, pipelines, and tools.

item.title: Blue Origin still doesn’t know why its New Glenn rocket blew up last month
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: But the company is still claiming that New Glenn will return to flight this year.
